name: First Arkansas Bank And Trust
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank serving Arkansas since 1949, offering consumer checking, business accounts, lending, and wealth management with local service.
description_long:
First Arkansas Bank & Trust (FAB&T) was founded in 1949 by Kenneth Pat Wilson and has operated as a locally-owned and managed institution throughout its seven-decade history. The bank serves customers across Arkansas from rural towns to the state capital, maintaining a community-first approach while modernizing its service delivery. The organization has been recognized multiple times as a Best Places to Work winner by Arkansas Business and has received endorsements from the U.S. Department of Defense, Forbes, and The Institute for Extraordinary Banking.

FAB&T offers a comprehensive suite of consumer and business banking products. For consumers, they provide checking and savings accounts, credit cards, home equity lines of credit, and mobile banking with Zelle integration for peer-to-peer transfers. Business customers can access business checking accounts, business services, and lending solutions. The bank also provides wealth management and investment advisory services, along with online banking and a digital teller service called QŪB for remote banking assistance.

The bank distinguishes itself through its emphasis on community values and local decision-making. Their stated core values include being respectful, following the "Do-Right Rule," following through on commitments, holding each other accountable, and making banking fun. They offer convenience features like online account opening, account switching assistance, mobile deposit capabilities, and financial calculators. The organization actively invests in community involvement and maintains multiple physical branch locations throughout Arkansas.

As a traditional community bank, FAB&T serves customers seeking personalized, local banking with FDIC insurance protection. However, the website provides limited specific information about product terms, rates, fees, or comprehensive service comparisons. Customers should contact the bank directly for detailed pricing information on specific products like checking accounts, credit cards, and lending products. The bank's strength lies in local service and community commitment rather than digital-first innovation compared to larger regional or national competitors. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Locally-owned and managed since 1949 with seven decades of community banking history
Multiple recognition as Best Places to Work by Arkansas Business
Zelle integration for fast, safe peer-to-peer money transfers via mobile app
QŪB digital teller service for instant remote banking assistance
Comprehensive offerings including checking, savings, credit cards, HELOC, business services, and wealth management
Mobile banking with deposit capture and fund transfer capabilities
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to Arkansas only
Website provides minimal specific information on interest rates, fees, or product terms
No mention of competitive rates or comparative advantages over larger regional banks
Limited details on business loan products, SBA offerings, or commercial lending services
best_for: Arkansas residents seeking personalized community banking with local decision-making
Small business owners in Arkansas looking for relationship-based banking services
Customers who value established institutions with strong community involvement
diagnosis: First Arkansas Bank & Trust is best for Arkansas residents and business owners seeking community-focused banking with personal service, local decision-making, and FDIC protection. Primary caveat: limited geographic availability (Arkansas only) and sparse website information on rates, fees, and specific product terms require direct contact with the bank for comparison shopping and detailed pricing.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
